few-shot: Write a comprehensive guide to sustainable urban farming. The guide must cover at least 4 different farming methods, include specific cost estimates in USD for each method, address common failures and how to avoid them, maintain an encouraging tone for beginners throughout, and end with a seasonal planting calendar for temperate climates.

Draft a proposal for a new employee wellness program at a mid-size tech company. The proposal must include at least 3 program components with implementation timelines, cite at least 2 statistical benefits of workplace wellness programs, provide a monthly budget breakdown under $5000, address potential employee resistance with specific counterarguments, and include measurable KPIs for evaluating the program after 6 months.

Write an in-depth article comparing remote work and office work cultures. The article must present at least 3 arguments for each side with supporting evidence, include quotes or perspectives from at least 2 different industries, maintain a neutral and balanced tone throughout without revealing a personal preference, address the impact on mental health with specific studies or statistics, and conclude with a practical framework that companies can use to decide which model fits them best.

A factory produces three products A, B, and C. Product A requires 2 hours of machining and 1 hour of assembly, product B requires 1 hour of machining and 3 hours of assembly, and product C requires 3 hours of machining and 2 hours of assembly. The factory has 120 hours of machining time and 150 hours of assembly time available per week. Profit per unit is $40 for A, $60 for B, and $50 for C. Formulate this as a linear programming problem, define all variables and constraints explicitly, solve for the optimal production quantities step by step, verify your solution satisfies all constraints, and calculate the maximum weekly profit.

In a round-robin chess tournament with 6 players, every player plays exactly one game against every other player. After the tournament, the results show: Player 1 won 4 games, Player 2 won 3 games, Player 3 won 3 games, and Player 4 won 2 games. Determine the number of wins for Players 5 and 6, prove that your answer is the only valid solution, identify all possible head-to-head outcomes that are consistent with the final standings, explain whether a three-way tie for second place is possible under these constraints, and verify your answer by confirming the total number of games played equals the expected count.

A train leaves Station A heading toward Station B at 80 km/h. Thirty minutes later, another train leaves Station B heading toward Station A at 100 km/h. The distance between the two stations is 450 km. A bird starts flying from Station A at the same time as the first train, traveling at 120 km/h back and forth between the two approaching trains. Calculate when and where the two trains will meet, determine the total distance the bird flies before the trains meet, show all intermediate calculations step by step, verify your answer by checking both trains' distances sum to 450 km, and explain what happens to the bird's individual trip lengths as the trains get closer together.

Write a Python command-line tool that converts CSV files to JSON format. The tool must accept input and output file paths as command-line arguments using argparse, handle missing values by replacing them with null in the JSON output, support an optional --pretty flag for indented JSON output, include proper error handling for file not found and malformed CSV with user-friendly error messages, add logging that writes to both console and a log file, and include at least 3 unit tests covering normal operation and edge cases.

Build a Python class called TaskScheduler that manages a priority-based task queue. The scheduler must support adding tasks with a name, priority (1-10), and estimated duration in minutes, implement both FIFO and priority-based scheduling modes switchable at runtime, include a method that returns the optimal execution order minimizing total wait time, raise custom exceptions for invalid inputs such as duplicate task names or out-of-range priorities, provide a summary method that returns statistics including total tasks, average priority, and estimated completion time, and include type hints throughout with a comprehensive docstring for each method.

Write a Python script that analyzes a directory of text files and generates a word frequency report. The script must recursively scan all .txt files in the given directory and subdirectories, normalize words by converting to lowercase and removing punctuation, exclude a configurable list of stop words loaded from an external file, output results sorted by frequency in descending order in both CSV and plain text formats, support a --top-n flag to limit output to the N most frequent words, handle encoding errors gracefully by skipping unreadable files and logging warnings, and display a progress bar during processing.

A retail company's quarterly sales data shows the following: Q1 revenue was $2.3M with 45% gross margin, Q2 was $1.8M with 38% gross margin, Q3 was $2.7M with 51% gross margin, and Q4 was $3.1M with 47% gross margin. Operating expenses were flat at $600K per quarter. Analyze the revenue trend and identify which quarter underperformed and propose at least 2 likely causes, calculate net profit for each quarter and identify the most profitable one, determine whether the gross margin variation correlates with revenue volume and explain why or why not, project Q1 of next year using at least two different forecasting approaches, and summarize your findings in an executive summary of no more than 200 words.

A city's public transportation survey collected responses from 5,000 residents. Key findings: 62% use buses, 28% use subway, 10% use trams. Average commute satisfaction is 3.2/5 for buses, 4.1/5 for subway, and 3.8/5 for trams. Average commute time is 47 minutes for bus users, 31 minutes for subway users, and 35 minutes for tram users. The city's transportation budget is $50M annually. Analyze the relationship between mode usage, satisfaction, and commute time, identify the most cost-effective mode to expand assuming subway expansion costs 3x more per rider than bus, recommend a budget allocation across the three modes with specific dollar amounts and justification, address equity concerns for neighborhoods that only have bus access, and present your analysis with at least 3 specific data-derived insights that aren't immediately obvious from the raw numbers.

An e-commerce platform's A/B test results for a new checkout flow are as follows: Control group (n=12,000) had a 3.2% conversion rate with average order value of $67. Treatment group (n=11,500) had a 3.8% conversion rate with average order value of $61. The test ran for 14 days. Determine whether the conversion rate difference is statistically significant, explain the tradeoff between higher conversion and lower order value, calculate the projected annual revenue impact of adopting the new flow assuming 500,000 monthly visitors, identify at least 2 potential confounding variables that could invalidate the results, recommend whether to ship the new flow and under what conditions, and explain your reasoning in terms a non-technical product manager would understand.

A software startup with 8 developers needs to plan a 12-week product roadmap. They have three major features to build: user authentication (estimated 4 weeks, requires 3 developers), payment integration (estimated 5 weeks, requires 4 developers), and analytics dashboard (estimated 6 weeks, requires 3 developers). Two developers are specialists who can only work on payment integration. Create a detailed week-by-week schedule that minimizes total delivery time, identify and resolve all resource conflicts explicitly, account for a 2-week buffer for unexpected delays without extending the 12-week timeline, specify which developers are assigned to which feature each week, and evaluate the risk of missing the deadline with a brief analysis of the critical path.

Plan a 5-day international academic conference for 300 attendees with a total budget of $150,000. The conference must include 4 keynote sessions, 20 parallel workshop slots across 4 tracks, and 2 networking events. Create a detailed daily schedule from 8 AM to 8 PM with specific time slots for each session, allocate the budget across venue, catering, speaker fees, materials, and contingency with specific dollar amounts, address logistical challenges for attendees from different time zones with at least 2 accommodations, include a backup plan for speaker cancellations, and design the workshop track themes so they progress in difficulty from day 1 to day 5.

A restaurant chain is planning to open 3 new locations over the next 18 months in different neighborhoods. Each location requires: site selection (2 months), permit acquisition (3 months, can overlap with renovation planning), renovation (4 months), staff hiring and training (2 months, must happen after renovation begins), and soft launch (1 month before grand opening). Create a Gantt-chart-style timeline for all three locations with specific month ranges, identify which phases can run in parallel across locations and which share resources, allocate a team of 2 project managers and 1 construction supervisor across all three projects without conflicts, calculate the minimum total timeline if Location 2 must open before Location 3, and identify the top 3 risks with specific mitigation strategies for each.
